viewer.appOnly: Preview is available in the desktop app

onboarding.sub: A local-first knowledge base with a built-in AI layer. Open a vault to begin — everything stays on your machine.
onboarding.vaultSub: A folder with your notes — everything stays local, in plain Markdown files.
onboarding.vaultOpenSub: System dialog; you can create a new folder right there.
onboarding.vaultDemoSub: Demo storage for the browser preview.
onboarding.aiSub: Chat model from .nexus/local.json (editable in Settings → AI / Models).
onboarding.aiNote: Local-first: the index and embeddings live on your device. You can set up AI later — the app fully works without it.
onboarding.indexSub: Runs in the background — you can enter right away; progress shows in the status bar.
onboarding.indexDoneSub: The vault is indexed. Welcome to Orvin.

dataSec.title: Backup & restore
dataSec.sub: Export your second brain (facts, conversations, episodes, agent skills) to a JSON file, or import one back with dedup.
dataSec.orphaned: ⚠ {{count}} item(s) had no matching conversation and were dropped (broken/hand-edited backup).
dataSec.schemaOld: ⚠ Backup is from an older schema — imported, but verify your data.

gen.userNameHint: Used only in the Home greeting; stored locally.

ed.readableWidthDesc: Caps text width for comfortable reading (like Obsidian).
ed.linkLivePreviewDesc: Hide `[[ ]]` and show the link name in the editor and in preview; in the editor the cursor reveals the full form.

hk.intro: Reassign command shortcuts. Changes are saved locally.
hk.conflict: Conflict: combo used by another command

aiSec.intro: Endpoints of local OpenAI-compatible servers. Stored in .nexus/local.json (outside git) — keys won't be synced.
aiSec.chatDesc: Answers in the AI chat (General and note-grounded modes). Applied immediately, no restart.
aiSec.embDesc: Vectorizes notes for semantic search and link suggestions. Changing it requires a restart and re-indexing.
aiSec.restart: Saved. Restart the app — embedding changed (re-indexing needed).
aiSec.fastDesc: A small model for short tasks: inline generation, contradiction judge, reasoning summary, news. Empty → the chat model is used.
aiSec.chatDeepDesc: “Deep”: the model reasons (chain-of-thought) before answering — sharper on rare complex questions, but +30–40 s per answer. Off = “Fast” (measured: reasoning did not improve quality for knowledge-base retrieval).
aiSec.rerankDesc: The fast model reorders retrieved fragments before answering: sharper citations, +~2 s per question. Golden-set: nDCG 0.88 → 1.00.
aiSec.chatMemoryDesc: Mix relevant snippets from past conversations into the answer's context (your "second brain"). Separate index — does not affect note search.
aiSec.agentMemoryDesc: Mix saved EXPLICIT FACTS about you and your projects (pinned + relevant) into AI answers. Off by default — privacy first. Manage facts below.
aiSec.memoryConsolidationDesc: When you confirm a fact, the AI suggests merging it with a similar one or replacing an outdated one (showing «before → after»). Every merge/replace goes through your confirmation and is reversible. Off by default; requires agent memory on.
aiSec.consolidationModeDesc: «Propose» — every merge/replace via a chip. «Auto» — apply silently (except your EXPLICIT facts — always via a chip), with «Undo» in the toast.
aiSec.episodicMemoryDesc: Mix short summaries of your past sessions («what we talked about before») into AI answers. Off by default — privacy. Manage episodes below.
aiSec.insightsDesc: Proactive AI widgets on Home: open questions, context drift, stale-note radar. Generated in the background on a schedule. Off by default — enable if your notes give them something to work with.
aiSec.contradictionsDesc: AI looks for pairs of notes that contradict each other (stale plans, conflicting facts). Background pass on a schedule. Off by default — niche and costly, enable when needed.
aiSec.explainRelationsDesc: In Related/Similar, show a short LLM explanation of how the notes relate instead of the raw snippet (lazy, cached). Without a utility model — falls back to the snippet.

egress.title: Network (egress)
egress.intro: Outbound connection policy for the core. Applies instantly and survives restarts (stored outside the vault and git).
egress.offlineDesc: Requests to public hosts are blocked before the socket; local and LAN models keep working. An active chat reply is cut off.
egress.chatDesc: Chat model requests (RAG chat, digest, inline).
egress.embedDesc: Vectorization of notes and queries (semantic search, RAG).
egress.probeDesc: Probe requests from “Test connection” to model endpoints.

web.title: Web agent (search)
web.intro: The chat's “Web” mode searches the internet via your self-hosted SearXNG. Requests go ONLY to the host you set; they're scanned for secrets before sending (W4).
web.urlHint: Your SearXNG instance address (with ?format=json support). Saving = consent to egress to this host.
web.enableDesc: A third chat mode “Web”: the model decides if internet is needed, searches and answers with sources.

skills.title: Self-learning & skills
skills.intro: When on, the agent can author its own SKILL.md files (through the approval gate, never auto). Off by default. Below: the skills folder and the agent's learned skills.
skills.learningDesc: Allow the agent to create/update skills. Owner-gated, off by default.
skills.noDir: No skills folder set — point it at a folder of SKILL.md files (e.g. .nexus/skills) to see the agent's skills here.
skills.empty: No skills yet — they appear as the agent learns.
skills.parseErrors: ⚠ {{count}} SKILL.md file(s) couldn't be parsed and were skipped.

agent.title: Autonomous (server) agent
agent.intro: Settings for the autonomous agent run via the connector (e.g. the nexus-agentd server). Stored in .nexus/local.json. The desktop AI chat and agent panel are NOT affected — they pick autonomy per run and use the web settings above.
agent.autonomyDesc: Default posture for the headless connector. «Confirm» — every change is proposed for approval. «Auto» — the agent auto-applies low-risk changes; risky or large writes are still proposed.
agent.autonomyWarn: Auto: the server agent applies low-risk changes without asking. Risky or large writes are still proposed; everything is logged and reversible.
agent.actuatorDesc: Let the agent actually create and edit notes (through the approval gate). Off — the agent only reads and answers; write tools are stubs.
agent.actuatorWarn: The agent can modify notes. Each change goes through approval (when autonomy is «Confirm») and is reversible (undo).
agent.sandboxDesc: Run the agent inside an ephemeral rootless container with no network. Required for shell execution.
agent.linuxOnly: Linux-only (rootless Podman) — inert on this platform.
agent.shellDesc: Let the agent run shell/process/git commands inside the sandbox. Always under Confirm — never Auto.
agent.shellReq: Requires the OS sandbox (Linux). Enable the sandbox first.
agent.shellWarn: The server agent may execute commands inside the sandbox. Each run needs explicit approval (Confirm, never Auto); a kill-switch and audit log apply.
agent.publicFetchDesc: Allow the agent's web.fetch to reach ANY public URL, not just your SearXNG. Needs the agent's web tools enabled in local.json.
agent.publicFetchWarn: The server agent may fetch any public URL. Private/loopback addresses stay blocked (SSRF guard); every request is audited.
agent.delegationDesc: The agent may spawn child subagents for parallel subtasks (flat, no nesting; conservative caps).
agent.delegationWarn: Enabled: the agent spawns subagents. Their actions still pass the same confirmation gate and blast-radius cap; writes inherit the parent's actuator posture.
agent.researchDesc: The agent may run multi-round web research and write a cited report. Requires delegation, the agent's web tools, and the actuator (to write the report) — all enabled.
agent.researchWarn: Enabled: the agent can run deep web research and write a report note. It also needs delegation + web tools + the actuator on, otherwise the tool stays inert. The report write passes the same confirmation gate and blast-radius cap.
agent.capabilitiesIntro: What the server agent is allowed to do. Everything off by default.

conn.title: Connection
conn.intro: The current vault's LLM endpoints and their reachability.
conn.modeDesc: Where the agent runs: embedded (in-app), an external daemon, or an ACP agent.
conn.acpCommandHint: Command that launches the ACP agent (e.g. "hermes acp"). Split on whitespace — quotes and environment variables are not supported.
conn.acpRemoteCommandHint: Command that starts the ACP agent ON THE REMOTE HOST (e.g. "docker exec -i hermes hermes acp"). Split on whitespace — quotes and environment variables are not supported.
conn.acpCwdHint: Directory the agent process is launched from. Empty → the current vault root.
conn.localWarn: Local mode: the agent runs one-shot — no conversation history and no per-run autonomy (the daemon uses its own config). The agentd daemon must open the SAME vault, otherwise edits go to the wrong place. Restarting agentd drops the current run — start the task again.
conn.acpWarn: ACP mode: the app launches an external ACP agent and hands it the turn. You get the connected agent's brain (its model, tools, skills), not the embedded Castor. Action requests go through your approval; the agent performs file operations itself in its working directory — point it at the intended vault root. Each turn is a separate one-shot run with no conversation memory; pause/resume and turn undo are not supported. Ending the turn terminates the agent process.

news.title: News
news.intro: Configure the AI news feed: which model analyzes items, sources, the keyword filter and allowed hosts.
news.enabledHint: Consent to collect news: once a day Orvin reaches out to the selected sources. Turning it off here disables the feed.
news.modelHint: Which model the feed uses to translate and summarize items: the fast utility model or the main one (higher quality, but slower).
news.modelUnset: Model not configured — set the endpoint in “AI / Models”.
news.keywordsHint: One per line or comma-separated. Empty — the default set.
news.extraHostsHint: Article hosts you allowed by clicking from the reader. Remove them here.
news.diagHint: Diagnostics, run history and logs live in the “News” feed.

sidebar.searchHint: Searches titles, paths and tags. FTS is instant · semantic search lives in the palette (⌘K).
sidebar.tagsEmpty: No tags yet — add a #tag to a note.
sidebar.starredEmpty: Nothing starred yet — hover a file in the tree and hit the star.

home.title: Home
home.searchPlaceholder: Search the vault or ask the assistant…
home.briefEmpty: No brief yet — hit “refresh” and the model will describe what's been happening.
home.briefNoChat: The daily brief needs a chat model. Configure it in Settings → AI (the “Chat model” field).
home.recentEmpty: Nothing yet — create your first note.
home.goalText: Best streak — **{{best}} d.** {{left}} d. to your record.
home.goalsEmpty: No goals. Add a #goal tag and a progress field to a note.
home.staleEmpty: Radar is clear — the vault is fresh.
home.staleDiscussPrompt: Help me decide what to do with the note “{{name}}” — {{count}} days untouched: update, archive, split, or delete?
home.oqEmpty: No questions yet — hit “refresh” and the model will find open threads in recent notes.
home.driftEmpty: No analysis yet — hit “refresh” and the model will compare your current focus with your goals.
home.insightsOff: Insights are off. Enable them in Settings → AI.
home.discussDriftPrompt: How do I refocus on my goals? Here's the current drift: {{drift}}

tagSuggest.flushFailed: Save the note first — write cancelled

tree.label: Vault files
tree.deleteConfirm: Move “{{name}}” to trash? You can restore it from .nexus/.trash.
tree.welcomeBody:
# Welcome to Orvin

This is your first note. A few basics:

- Link notes with [[wiki links]].
- Tag with #ideas.
- Open the local graph (Cmd/Ctrl+G) and the AI chat (Cmd/Ctrl+J).

Delete this note anytime — everything stays local on your machine.

versions.title: Version history
versions.noFile: Open a note to see its history
versions.identical: This version matches the current text
versions.pickHint: Pick a version on the left to see the differences

editor.selectFile: Pick a file in the tree, or press {{shortcut}}

external.title: File changed on disk while you have unsaved edits

inspector.title: Note inspector
inspector.summaryEmpty: No summary (needs an AI model in Settings → AI)

mermaid.error: Couldn’t render the Mermaid diagram (check the syntax)

capture.title: Quick capture
capture.placeholder: Jot a thought — Enter adds it to your Inbox…

templates.title: New from template
templates.empty: No templates. Create .md files in the Templates/ folder.

graph.title: Graph
graph.empty: Open a file to see its graph
graph.orphanSub: No backlinks — not connected to the rest of the graph.

status.awaiting: Waiting for your decision on changes

composer.tip: Nothing is written without your confirmation.

empty.title: Give the agent a task
empty.hint: The agent will plan, call tools and propose changes — you confirm each one.

changeset.title: Changes
changeset.footAuto: Auto mode · changes applied by the agent

plan.empty: No steps yet — the agent hasn't taken any actions in this run.

graph.title: Execution graph
graph.empty: The graph will appear as the agent acts.

report.empty: The report will appear when the run completes.

chat.title: AI panel
chat.modeVaultHint: Answer grounded in your notes (RAG + sources)
chat.modeGeneralHint: General chat with the model — no notes access
chat.localHint: Answers run on your local model
chat.empty: Ask something about your notes — the answer cites the sources it used.
chat.ask1: How does RAG work in Orvin?
chat.memoryCaptureHint: Save a fact from this answer to AI memory
chat.memoryNothing: Nothing to save from this answer
chat.noActiveNote: Open a note to insert into
chat.insertNeedsEdit: Switch to edit mode to insert
chat.pinHint: Pin the active note into AI context

denied.offlineSub: Requests to public hosts are blocked by the network policy. Turn it off in Settings → AI / Models → Network (egress).
denied.featureSub: The “chat” network feature is not enabled. Enable it in Settings → AI / Models → Network (egress).
denied.hostSub: The model endpoint is outside the core egress policy. Check the URL in Settings → AI / Models.
denied.secretSub: A secret was detected in the search query — it was not sent over the network (W4).
denied.notConfigured: Web search is not set up
denied.notConfiguredSub: Set the SearXNG URL in Settings → AI / Models → "Web agent (search)" and enable it.

castor.desc: Multi-step tasks in your vault — in a dedicated workspace. Changes only after your confirmation.
castor.seed1: Process my inbox notes: group them by topic and suggest where each should go.
castor.seed2: Find related notes and propose the [[…]] links that are missing between them.
castor.seed3: Tidy up my tags: find duplicate and near-synonym tags and suggest how to merge them.

chat.modeWebHint: Web agent: the model searches the internet via your SearXNG and answers with sources.
chat.historyEmpty: Nothing yet — ask your first question.

suggest.title: Suggested links
suggest.noFile: Open a note to see suggested links.

related.title: Related notes
related.noFile: Open a note to see related ones.

goals.title: Goals
goals.empty: No goals yet. Add a #goal tag and a progress field (0–100) to a note.

episode.title: Episodes
episode.empty: No episodes yet. The AI summarizes finished conversations in the background when episodic memory is on.
episode.purgeConfirm: Delete episode «{{title}}» forever? Its summary and vector will be erased (the session and messages remain).

memory.title: AI memory
memory.empty: Memory is empty. Add a fact about you or your projects manually — or confirm an auto-suggestion in chat.
memory.addPlaceholder: A new fact about you or a project…
memory.deleteConfirm: Delete the fact “{{text}}” from AI memory?
memory.overCap: Memory is over capacity ({{count}} of {{cap}}). Old facts are highlighted — delete the ones you don't need.

digest.title: Changes digest
digest.empty: No digest yet. Click “Generate” — the model will briefly summarize recent changes.
digest.queued: Generation started — the digest will appear here once the model responds.

contradictions.title: Contradiction finder
contradictions.empty: No contradictions found. Click “Find” — the model will compare topically close notes.
contradictions.disabled: Contradiction finder is off. Enable it in Settings → AI.
contradictions.queued: Search started — results will appear here once the model finishes.

news.title: News feed
news.ctaSub: Once a day Orvin gathers trusted sources, filters the noise, and gives you Russian headlines, summaries and a daily digest — read without leaving the app.
news.consentBody_one: Requests will go to {{count}} trusted source:
news.consentBody_other: Requests will go to {{count}} trusted sources:

reader.footTranslated: Full text translated by a local model. Original at the link.
reader.footOriginalRu: Russian-language source — text extracted from the original without translation.
reader.footDenied: The article lives on a host outside the trusted sources — the egress policy makes no request. Showing the summary; full version at the “Original” link.
reader.footTruncated: Text truncated by the length cap — full version at the “Original” link.
reader.allowHint: Only this host becomes trusted. Remove it from the feed's ⚙ menu.

related.title: Related notes
related.openFailed: Couldn't open the note — it may have been moved or deleted.

news.emptySub: Auto-run happens once a day while the vault is open. You can refresh manually.
news.caughtUpSub: No unread items in this filter.
news.errorSub: Previous data is kept. Check your connection and retry.
news.neverSub: Start the first run — fetching and summarizing takes about a minute.
news.offlineBanner: Offline — feed paused. Showing last run's data.
news.stalled: The scheduler is not responding: the job is queued but never runs. Restart the app — diagnostics are written to the log (Library/Application Support/app.nexus.desktop/logs).

diag.title: Diagnostics
diag.historyEmpty: History is empty — the feed has never run.
diag.emptyLlm: Analyzer unavailable: {{endpoint}} — entries were not scored. The feed won't update until the endpoint is fixed (Settings → AI).
diag.emptyLlmGeneric: The news analyzer was unavailable in the last run — entries were not scored.
diag.emptySources: Sources didn't respond in the last run ({{ok}} of {{total}}) — check your network and connection.
diag.emptyDedup: No fresh entries: everything the sources returned was already in the feed (dedup).
diag.emptyUnknown: The last run brought no new entries.

inline.ready: Suggestion ready — Tab to accept, Esc to dismiss
inline.errNoText: Place the cursor after some text — nothing to continue

ai.placeholder: Ask AI or describe what to insert…
ai.label: AI insert

deadJobs.title: Background tasks
deadJobs.hint: These tasks ran out of retries. Fix the cause (usually the model URL in Settings → AI / Models) and hit “Retry”, or clear the list.
deadJobs.empty: Empty: queue is clear, no errors.
deadJobs.restartHint: Respawn the background worker (if jobs are stuck) — no app restart needed.

plugins.title: Plugin manager
plugins.subtitle: Each plugin is isolated in a sandbox iframe. Access is request-based.
plugins.auditSub: Every plugin broker call is logged.
plugins.auditEmpty: No host calls yet — launch a plugin to interact with it.
plugins.privacyNote: Plugins get no network or file access until you explicitly allow it.
plugins.revocableNote: Consent is revocable anytime from the plugin card.

vault_read.title: Read notes
vault_read.desc: Reads vault files within the listed scopes.

vault_write.title: Write notes
vault_write.desc: Modifies vault files within the listed scopes.

ai_complete.title: Text generation
ai_complete.desc: Model requests on behalf of the plugin.

net.title: Network access
net.desc: Outbound requests to the listed hosts only.

conflict.title: Resolve conflicts
conflict.upToDate: Nothing to merge — already up to date
conflict.subtitle: This note changed here and on disk. Pick a version for each file.

board.title: Board
board.corruptConfig: Board config is broken — using defaults

dnd.statusError: Couldn't change status (broken frontmatter?) — card reverted

peek.title: Task preview
peek.empty: This note has no body yet.

empty.title: No tasks yet
empty.body: A task is a note with a “status” property. Add status: to a note's frontmatter and it shows up here as a card.

promote.outOfScope: Note became a “{{column}}” task, but outside this board's filter
promote.noNote: No open note to add to the board

prop.flushError: Save the note first (it has unsaved changes)

commands.memory.noSelection: Select text to save to memory

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.empty: No tasks yet. Add “- [ ] …” to a note or insert via “/”.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.empty: Inbox is empty. Capture thoughts with ⌘⇧N — they show up here to process.
